evidence: The rapid evolution of AI may require us to allocate additional resources to help implement AI ethically... and may also require us to make additional investments in the development of proprietary datasets, machine learning models or other systems
investing in the transition to operating high-power fast-charging infrastructure for autonomous vehicles
Failure to manage and successfully integrate acquired businesses and technologies, including managing internal controls and any privacy, data security or AI risks associated with such acquisitions
shifts in consumer behavior, including the use of new or emerging technologies, such as AI-enabled platforms and digital assistants, may disintermediate our relationship with consumers
Our growing use of AI (including machine learning) in our business and offerings presents additional risks
AI algorithms or automated decision-making or processing of data may be flawed and datasets may be insufficient or contain inaccurate or biased information

AI algorithms or automated decision-making or processing of data may be flawed... can create inaccurate or discriminatory outcomes. AI tools and algorithms may use third-party AI with unclear intellectual property rights

Europe, the United States and other countries are enacting or may consider comprehensive legal compliance frameworks specifically for AI... the European Artificial Intelligence Act (AI Act), which came into effect in August 2024

AI use or management by us or others... have impaired and could impair the acceptance of AI solutions or subject us to lawsuits, regulatory investigations or other harm, such as negative impacts to the value of our intellectual property or our brand

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified dollar amounts or headcount figures tied specifically to AI investment are disclosed in the excerpts; named AI investment areas are referenced but without scale, limiting score to 3.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Excerpts are predominantly risk-factor and MD&A boilerplate; no deployed AI use cases with operational detail or metrics are described. Named uses (automated decision-making, ML models) lack deployment status or outcomes.
capex_mentions: No specific dollar figures for AI-attributable capital expenditure appear in the excerpts.
Excerpts are heavily weighted toward risk factors; limited business/strategy sections may underrepresent actual AI deployment narrative present elsewhere in the full filing.
